elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper, we obtain order estimates for the Gelfand widths of intersections of finite-dimensional balls under some conditions on parameters.
elements.sent-2411-002: In this paper, we consider the problem of estimating the Gelfand $n$-widths of an intersection of finite-dimensional balls for $n\le aN$, where $a\in (0, \, 1/2]$ is an absolute constant.
elements.sent-2411-003: In some previous articles, the similar problem was considered for the Kolmogorov widths \cite{galeev1, vas_ball_inters .}
elements.sent-2411-004: For some conditions on the parameters, we obtain the order estimates for the Gelfand widths; as a consequence, under some conditions on the parameters we get the order estimates for linear widths.
elements.sent-2411-005: Let us give the definitions of the Gelfand, Kolmogorov and linear widths.
elements.sent-2411-006: Let $X$ be a linear normed space.
elements.sent-2411-007: By $X^*$ we denote the dual space for $X$, by ${\cal L _n(X)$, the family of all linear subspaces in $X$ of dimension at most $n$, by $L(X, \, X)$, the space of linear continuous operators on $X$.}
elements.sent-2411-008: For $A\in L(X, \, X)$, by ${\rm rk}\, A$ we denote the dimension of the range of $A$.
elements.sent-2411-009: Let $M\subset X$ be a nonempty set, $n\in \Z_+$.
elements.sent-2411-010: The Gelfand, Kolmogorov and linear $n$-widths of the set $M$ in $X$ are defined, correspondingly, as follows: \begin{equation}d^n(M, \, X) = \inf _{x_1^*, \, \dots, \, x_n^*\in X^*} \sup \Bigl\{\|x\|:\; x\in M\cap \Bigl(\cap _{j=1}^n \ker x_j^*\Bigr)\Bigr\};\end{equation} \begin{equation}d_n(M, \, X) = \inf _{L\in {\cal L}_n(X)} \sup _{x\in M} \inf _{y\in L} \|x-y\|;\end{equation} \begin{equation}\lambda_n(M, \, X) = \inf _{A\in L(X, \, X), \, {\rm rk}\, A\le n} \sup _{x\in M} \|x-Ax\|.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-001: \begin{equation}d^n(M, \, X) = \inf _{x_1^*, \, \dots, \, x_n^*\in X^*} \sup \Bigl\{\|x\|:\; x\in M\cap \Bigl(\cap _{j=1}^n \ker x_j^*\Bigr)\Bigr\};\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-003: \begin{equation}\lambda_n(M, \, X) = \inf _{A\in L(X, \, X), \, {\rm rk}\, A\le n} \sup _{x\in M} \|x-Ax\|.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-011: From the definitions it follows that $d_n(M, \, X)\le \lambda_n(M, \, X)$, $d^n(M, \, X) \le \lambda_n(M, \, X)$.
elements.sent-2411-013: We set $\|(x_i)_{i=1 ^N\|_{l_p^N = \left(\sum \limits _{i=1 ^N |x_i|^p\right)^{1/p}$ for $p<\infty$, $\|(x_i)_{i=1 ^N\|_{l_p^N = \max _{1\le i\le N |x_i|$ by $p=\infty$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: The space $\mathbb{R ^N$ with this norm is denoted by $l_p^N$.}
elements.sent-2411-015: By $B_p^N$ we denote the unit ball in $l_p^N$.
elements.sent-2411-016: Given $1\le p\le \infty$, we denote by $p'$ the dual exponent of $p$ defined by the equation $\frac{1 {p + \frac{1 {p' =1$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-017: The problem of estimating $d_n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)$, $d^n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)$ and $\lambda_n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)$ was studied in \cite{k_p_s, stech_poper, pietsch1, stesin, kashin_oct, bib_kashin, kashin_matr, gluskin1, bib_gluskin, garn_glus .}
elements.sent-2411-018: For $p\ge q$, the sharp values of these widths are known \cite{pietsch1, stesin , as well as the sharp values of $d_n(B_1^N, \, l_2^N)= \lambda_n(B_1^N, \, l_2^N)$ \cite{k_p_s, stech_poper .}}
elements.sent-2411-019: Hence from the duality formulas $d_n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)=d^n(B_{q' ^N, \, l_{p' ^N)$, $\lambda_n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)=\lambda_n(B_{q' ^N, \, l_{p' ^N)$ (see \cite{ioffe_tikh ) we get the sharp values of $d^n(B_2^N, \, l_\infty^N)=\lambda_n(B_2^N, \, l_\infty^N)$.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-020: Order estimates for $d_n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)$, $d^n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)$ and $\lambda_n(B_p^N, \, l_q^N)$ are obtained, correspondingly, for $\{1\le p\le q\le \infty:\; q<\infty\; \text{or (q=\infty,\; p\ge 2)\}$, $\{1\le p\le q\le \infty:\; p>1\, \text{or (p=1,\; q\le 2)\}$, $\{1\le p\le q\le \infty:\; (p, \, q)\ne (1, \, \infty)\}$ (see \cite{bib_kashin, kashin_matr, gluskin1, bib_gluskin, garn_glus ).}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: In \cite{galeev1 order estimates for $d_n(\cap _{\alpha\in A}\nu_\alpha B^N_{p_\alpha , \, l_q^N)$ were obtained for $N=2n$ (here $\nu_\alpha>0$, $\alpha\in A$).}}
elements.sent-2411-022: In \cite{vas_ball_inters , this result was generalized for the case $n\le N/2$; after that, the similar problem for mixed norms was studied \cite{vas_mix_sev .}}
elements.sent-2411-023: The order estimates of $d_n(\cap _{\alpha\in A}\nu_\alpha B^N_{p_\alpha , \, l_q^N)$ (where $n\le N/2$) were obtained for $1\le q<\infty$ and arbitrary $p_\alpha$, as well as for $q=\infty$ and $p_\alpha\ge 2$, $\alpha\in A$.}
elements.sent-2411-024: Also some simple cases were mentioned, when $\lambda_n(\cap _{\alpha\in A}\nu_\alpha B^N_{p_\alpha , \, l_q^N)$ and $d^n(\cap _{\alpha\in A}\nu_\alpha B^N_{p_\alpha , \, l_q^N)$ can be similarly estimated \cite[Remark 1]{vas_mix_sev .}}}
elements.sent-2411-025: In particular, for the Gelfand widths the order estimates can be easily written if 1) $p_\alpha\ge q$, $\alpha\in A$, 2) $2\le p_\alpha\le q$, $\alpha\in A$; they are given, correspondingly, by formulas $\inf _{\alpha\in A \nu_\alpha N^{1/q-1/p_\alpha}$ and $\inf _{\alpha\in A \nu_\alpha$.}}
elements.sent-2411-026: In this paper we consider two more areas of parameters, where we could obtain order estimates of $d^n(\cap _{\alpha\in A}\nu_\alpha B^N_{p_\alpha , \, l_q^N)$.}

abla^r f\|_{L_p(\Omega)}\le 1\}\end{equation} is the Sobolev class; here $\|
abla^r f\|_{L_p(\Omega)}$ is the $L_p$-norm of the function $|
abla^r f(\cdot)|$.

elements.sent-2411-281: In \cite{mal_rjut1 order estimates for the Kolmogorov widths of sets invariant with respect to $G$ in the space $l_q^N$ for $1\le q\le 2$ were obtained.}
elements.sent-2411-282: Let $\|\cdot \|_X$ be a norm on $\R^N$ invariant with respect to $G$; i.e., $\|gx\|_X = \|x\|_X$, $x\in X$, $g\in G$.
elements.sent-2411-283: Let $b=(b_1, \, \dots, \, b_N)\in \R^N$ be such that $x \stackrel{x^*_b {\mapsto \langle b, \, x\rangle$ is the supporting functional at $\hat x$; i.e., $\|x^*_b\|_{X^* =1$ and $\langle b, \, \hat x\rangle = \|\hat x\|_X$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-284: As in Lemma \ref{low_est_lem , we get from \eqref{quad_norm that \begin{equation}\label{dnvx2_low} (d_n(V, \, X))^2 \ge \frac{\|\hat x\|^2_X}{2} -2\|\hat x\|_X \cdot \frac{1}{|G|} \sum \limits _{g\in G} \langle gb, \, y_g \rangle + \frac{c}{|G|} \sum \limits _{g\in G}\|y_g\|^2_X.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-285: Notice that for $i\ne j$ we have $\langle \varphi_i, \, \varphi_j\rangle_{L_2(G) = 0$, and for $i=j$, \begin{equation}\langle \varphi_i, \, \varphi_i\rangle_{L_2(G)} = \frac{1}{|G|} \sum \limits _{g\in G} (gb)_i^2 = \frac{1}{|H|} \sum \limits _{h\in H} b_{h(i)}^2 = \frac{\|b\|^2_{l_2^N}}{N}\end{equation} (the last equality holds since $H$ acts transitively on $\{1, \, \dots, \, N\}$).}
elements.sent-2411-286: Since $\dim Z\le n$, the Hilbert--Schmidt norm of $P$ is at most $n^{1/2}$.. Hence \begin{equation}\Bigl(\sum \limits _{i=1}^N \|P\varphi_i\|^2\Bigr)^{1/2} \le \frac{n^{1/2}\|b\|_{l_2^N}}{N^{1/2}}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-287: Then there is an absolute constant $\alpha>0$ such that, for $n \le \alpha \frac{N {\|b\|^2_{l_2^N}\|I\|^2_{X \rightarrow l_2^N }$, \begin{equation}d^n(B_{X^*}, \, Y^*)=d_n(B_Y, \, X) \ge \frac{\|\hat x\|_X}{4}.\end{equation}.}}
